title: Ingenuity's Final Flight on Mars
story: Soaring through the thin Martian air, NASA's Ingenuity helicopter made history. This tiny aircraft became the first to fly on another planet in 2021. On January 18, 2024, after an incredible journey, Ingenuity's mission came to an end. It had flown 72 times, far surpassing expectations. Originally planned for just five flights, this plucky explorer kept going for three years, paving the way for future Mars missions.
activity_question: What was Ingenuity's main achievement?
activity_choices: Becoming the first aircraft to fly on another planet
Lasting for five years on Mars
activity_answer: Becoming the first aircraft to fly on another planet
follow_up_question: How many flights was Ingenuity originally expected to make?

title: Daring Duo Completes Extreme Parachute Challenge
story: Two thrill-seekers made history on January 18, 1981. Phil Smith and Phil Mayfield leaped from a towering Houston skyscraper, completing an incredible feat. They became the first people to BASE jump from all four types of objects: buildings, cliffs, bridges, and antennae. This dangerous sport involves parachuting from fixed structures. While exciting, it's crucial to remember that BASE jumping requires extensive training and is extremely risky.

title: Space Rock Crashes Through Missouri Home
story: Imagine a rock from outer space crashing through your roof! That's exactly what happened in 1916 near Baxter, Missouri. On January 18, a meteorite weighing about as much as a large book smashed into a house. This space visitor, called a chondrite, came from the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter. Luckily, no one was hurt by this unexpected cosmic delivery!

title: Space Rocks Make a Splash in Canada
story: Boom! On January 18, 2000, the sky lit up over northwestern British Columbia. Pieces of a meteorite, later named Tagish Lake, came crashing down to Earth. This wasn't just any space rock. Scientists discovered it was one of the most primitive meteorites ever found, giving us clues about the early solar system. It's like holding a piece of space history in your hands!

It was the largest meteorite to hit Earth
It was made of pure gold
activity_answer: It was one of the most primitive meteorites ever found

title: Cosmic Heartbeat Found in Space
story: Stargazers got a thrilling surprise in 1989! On January 18, astronomers detected a pulsar—a rapidly spinning star that pulses like a cosmic heartbeat—hidden in the remains of a massive stellar explosion. This celestial discovery happened in a nearby galaxy called the Large Magellanic Cloud, where a supernova had burst into view just two years earlier. Finding this pulsar helped scientists better understand how stars live and die.

title: Jim Thorpe's Olympic Triumph Restored
story: Justice prevailed for a legendary athlete on January 18, 1983. The International Olympic Committee finally returned Jim Thorpe's gold medals, stolen from him decades earlier. Thorpe, a Native American sports hero, had won both pentathlon and decathlon in 1912. But his victories were unfairly stripped away when it was discovered he'd earned a small amount playing semi-pro baseball. After years of campaigning, Thorpe's Olympic legacy was rightfully restored.

activity_question: What did David Bellamy become famous for?
activity_choices: Being a botanist and TV presenter
Inventing a new type of telescope
activity_answer: Being a botanist and TV presenter
follow_up_question: What do you think made David Bellamy good at teaching about nature on TV?

title: A Peacemaker's Birth in Northern Ireland
story: John Hume, a champion for peace, entered the world on January 18, 1937, in Derry, Northern Ireland. He grew up to become a powerful voice for unity, working tirelessly to bring people together. Hume's efforts led to a groundbreaking peace agreement and earned him a share of the Nobel Peace Prize.
activity_question: What prestigious award did John Hume receive for his peace efforts?
follow_up_question: Why do you think peace is important in places where people disagree?

title: Thompson Twins' Tom Bailey Rocks Into Life
story: Imagine a world without 'Hold Me Now'! That catchy tune might never have existed if Tom Bailey hadn't been born on January 18, 1954. This English rocker entered the world in Halifax, ready to make musical waves. Bailey grew up to become the voice and keyboard wizard of the Thompson Twins, creating hit songs that still make people dance today.
activity_question: What instrument did Tom Bailey play in the Thompson Twins?
bonus_fact: The Thompson Twins weren't actually twins - they were a band named after characters from a comic book!

title: From Wisconsin to the Stars
story: Reaching for the stars began in a small Wisconsin village for Jeffrey N Williams. Born in Superior on January 18, 1958, this future astronaut would grow up to join both the United States Army and NASA. Williams' journey from a Midwestern hometown to orbiting Earth shows how big dreams can take flight from anywhere.
activity_question: What two organizations did Jeffrey N Williams join?
activity_choices: The United States Army and NASA
activity_answer: The United States Army and NASA
follow_up_question: What do you think it takes to become an astronaut?

title: A Star is Born: Mark Rylance
story: Lights, camera, action! On January 18, 1960, a future star entered the world. Mark Rylance, the talented English actor and theatre director, was born in Ashford, Kent. Little did anyone know that this baby would grow up to dazzle audiences on stage and screen, even winning an Oscar for his role in Bridge of Spies!
activity_question: What award-winning film is Mark Rylance known for?
follow_up_question: Can you name another job Mark Rylance has besides acting?

title: Boxing Champion Virgil Hill Born
story: Pow! Jab! Hook! On January 18, 1964, a future boxing legend entered the ring of life. Virgil Hill, born in Clinton, Missouri, would grow up to become a powerhouse in the boxing world. He claimed multiple world titles, including the WBA light heavyweight championship. Hill's lightning-fast fists and strategic mind made him a force to be reckoned with in the ring.
activity_question: What boxing title did Virgil Hill win?
follow_up_question: What qualities do you think make a great boxer?
